task:
Survey the engram-harness codebase at C:\Users\Owner\code\personal\engram-harness for all mechanisms that handle long tool-call outputs — things like truncation, summarization, paging, output caps, streaming, sub-agent delegation, or any other strategy that prevents a single tool call from flooding the context window.

Specifically:
1. List the top-level directory structure
2. Find any files that mention: truncation, max_chars, output cap, token limit, summarize, paging, offset/limit on tool outputs, sub-agent / spawn, streaming
3. Look at the tool definitions (likely in a tools/ or schema directory) for parameters like offset, limit, max_chars, max_results, stat, etc. that let the caller bound output size
4. Look for any middleware or pipeline layer that post-processes tool results before returning them to the model
5. Check for any sub-agent / delegation mechanism (spawn_subagent or similar) that isolates large work from the main context

Return a structured summary of every mechanism you find, grouped by category (built-in tool parameters, output truncation middleware, sub-agent delegation, streaming, other). Include file paths and brief descriptions.
